title: Property names should not match get methods
description: Property names should not match get methodsAnalyze your codeconsistency-identifiablemaintainabilityCode SmellconfusingWhy is this an issue?Properties and Get method should have names that makes them clearly distinguishable.This rule raises an issue when the name of a public or protected member starts with 'Get' and otherwise matches the name of a public or protected property.Noncompliant code exampleusing System; namespace MyLibrary { public class Foo { public DateTime Date { get { return DateTime.Today; } } public string GetDate() // Noncompliant { return this.Date.ToString(); } } }Compliant solutionusing System; namespace MyLibrary { public class Foo { public DateTime Date { get { return DateTime.Today; } } public string GetDateAsString() { return this.Date.ToString(); } } }Available In:
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Uses custom function to compare property names with Get method names within the same class
logic.triggering_conditions: When a class has both a property named 'X' and a method named 'GetX' with the same accessibility level
exclusion_conditions: Methods and properties with different accessibility levels
Static methods vs instance properties or vice versa
Methods with significantly different return types indicating different purposes
Methods in different partial class parts
never_trigger_on: Private methods vs public properties (different accessibility)
Methods with different return types indicating different semantics
logic.reasoning: Uses a custom function to analyze class members and detect naming conflicts between properties and Get methods. The custom function 'check_property_method_name_conflicts' examines PropertyDeclaration and MethodDeclaration nodes to find cases where a property 'Name' coexists with a method 'GetName', which can cause confusion for API users. The function considers accessibility, scope, and context to avoid false positives.

should_trigger: public DateTime Date { get; } and public string GetDate()
protected string Name { get; set; } and protected string GetName()
public int Count { get; } and public int GetCount()
should_not_trigger: public DateTime Date { get; } and private string GetDate()
public static string GetVersion() and public string Version { get; }
public string Name { get; } and public Person GetName()
public override string ToString() and public string String { get; }
property and method in different classes
